page: Microsoft Azure NCC40ads_H100_v5 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the NCCads2023 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture AMD processor with 40 dedicated virtual CPUs. The instance is equipped with 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the instance includes a 1049 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance persistent disk attachments. Despite the family designation, this specific configuration contains zero GPUs. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is classified as memory-optimized. It is designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources, such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and data analytics pipelines. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention, making it a stable choice for enterprise-scale database hosting and memory-intensive applications.
description: Microsoft Azure NCC40ads_H100_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the NCCads2023 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 40 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of system memory, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a 1049 GB local disk alongside Premium Storage support for high-throughput persistent volumes. This instance configuration does not include any GPUs. Given its high memory density and dedicated compute allocation, the NCC40ads_H100_v5 is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database deployments, enterprise resource planning systems, and in-memory data analytics.
